title: leptodactylon pungens subsp. squarrosum
intro: Leptodactylon pungens subsp. squarrosum, also known as creeping sage, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and Mexico. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of creeping sage include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, creeping sage is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be applied topically in the form of salves or ointments to promote healing and reduce inflammation.
